hero.lead1: Discover, tag, run, and monitor hundreds of your .ahk scripts —

before.lead: You have scripts. You don't have a tool.
before.b3: tags and categories? only in your head
before.b4: no search, no overview — just folders and memory

after.lead: The same .ahk files, one window. Run or kill in one click — no double-click expedition, no tray-icon hunt.
after.b3: status, tags, name at a glance
after.b4: search by name, tag, or path — instant

vim.heading: Built keyboard-first.
vim.leadPart4: to run. Arrow keys and mouse still work — but you won't need them.

install.sub: Windows 10 or 11. ~8 MB. No account, no telemetry, no network calls.

faq.heading: Things people ask.
faq.q1: Is it safe? Does it send anything to the network?
faq.a1: Fully open-source under MIT. Zero telemetry, zero analytics, no update pings. autlas works offline — it only talks to the filesystem, AHK processes, and (optionally) a local Everything IPC pipe.
faq.q2: Do I need AutoHotkey installed separately?
faq.a2: Yes. autlas is a hub, not a bundler — install AHK once (v1.1+, v2, or both) and autlas picks up the runtime automatically from file associations.
faq.q3: Does it work with AHK v1 and v2?
faq.a3: Both. autlas reads the first few lines of each script and routes to the matching interpreter. You can override per-script from the context menu.
faq.a4Lead: In a local SQLite DB under
faq.a4Tail: , keyed by a stable script ID — not the path. Rename or move a file and your tags stick.
faq.q5: Does it scan my whole disk?
faq.a5: Only the paths you add in Settings. If Everything is installed, scans of 10k+ files finish in under a second via its IPC pipe; otherwise an in-process walker does the job.
faq.q6: Can I use it without any Vim knowledge?
faq.a6: Yes — vim mode is opt-in. Mouse, arrow keys, and a normal context menu cover everything, same as a regular Windows app.
faq.a7Lead: Not yet. autlas is Windows-only because AutoHotkey itself is Windows-only. A cross-platform
faq.a7Tail: (running plain shell/Python/Deno scripts) is on the roadmap.

finalCta.heading: Take control of your AutoHotkey scripts.
finalCta.sub: Free. Open-source. Windows-native. No account, no catch.
